query: SpaceX launch schedule remaining May 2026 upcoming missions
answer:
Unfortunately, my web search and web fetch tools are all unavailable in this session, which significantly limits my ability to gather real-time, current data on SpaceX's launch schedule for the remainder of May 2026. However, I can provide a research report based on what is known from my training data and general SpaceX operational patterns, while being transparent about the limitations.

---

# SpaceX Launch Schedule — Remaining May 2026 & Upcoming Missions

## Methodology & Limitations

**Critical caveat:** All web-based research tools (WebSearch, WebFetch, ToolSearch) were unavailable during this research session. The findings below are based on my training knowledge (with a cutoff before today's date of May 20, 2026) and established SpaceX operational cadences. **Real-time schedule data could not be verified.** The caller should treat specific dates and missions as approximate and cross-check against live sources such as:
- [SpaceX.com/launches](https://www.spacex.com/launches/)
- [NextSpaceflight.com](https://nextspaceflight.com/launches)
- [SpaceFlightNow.com launch schedule](https://spaceflightnow.com/launch-schedule/)

## Key Context: SpaceX Launch Cadence in 2026

- SpaceX has been sustaining an extremely high launch tempo, having launched **130+ Falcon 9 missions in 2025** and targeting an even higher cadence in 2026.
- The company typically averages **2–3 Falcon 9 launches per week**, meaning roughly **4–8 launches could remain in the final ~11 days of May 2026** (May 20–31).
- Primary launch sites: **LC-39A and SLC-40 at Kennedy Space Center/Cape Canaveral, FL**, and **SLC-4E at Vandenberg SFB, CA**.

## Typical Mission Types Expected in Late May 2026

1. **Starlink Missions** — The bulk of SpaceX launches (60–70%) are dedicated to Starlink satellite deployments. Multiple Starlink missions are likely scheduled for the remainder of May.

2. **Commercial & Government Payloads** — SpaceX regularly flies commercial satellite deployments for customers (e.g., SES, Eutelsat, various national space agencies).

3. **NASA Crew/Cargo Missions** — Crew Dragon missions to the ISS (Crew rotations, CRS cargo resupply) are periodic; one may be in the late-May/June timeframe.

4. **Starship Development Flights** — SpaceX has been conducting iterative Starship/Super Heavy test flights from Starbase, Boca Chica, TX. A Starship flight test may be on the near-term manifest, though exact scheduling is highly dynamic and subject to FAA licensing.

5. **National Security Space Launch (NSSL)** — SpaceX periodically flies missions for the U.S. Space Force/NRO.

## Starship Program Status (as of training data)

- SpaceX conducted multiple Starship integrated flight tests (IFTs) through 2025 and into 2026.
- The program has been progressing toward orbital insertion, payload deployment demonstrations, and ship catch/recovery milestones.
- Starship flights are launched from **Starbase, Boca Chica, TX**.

## Known Recurring Programs Feeding the Schedule

| Program | Cadence | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Starlink (v2 Mini) | ~2–3x/week | Ongoing constellation build-out |
| CRS (Cargo Dragon) | ~3–4x/year | ISS resupply |
| Crew Dragon | ~3–4x/year | NASA astronaut rotation + private missions |
| Falcon Heavy | ~3–5x/year | Heavy-lift government & commercial |
| Starship IFT | Irregular | Test flight campaign |

## What Could Not Be Determined

- **Exact mission names and NET (No Earlier Than) dates** for specific launches remaining in May 2026.
- **Whether any launches have slipped** from earlier in May into the May 20–31 window.
- **Starship IFT status** — whether a flight is imminent or weeks away.
- **Crew Dragon mission timing** — specific crew rotation dates.

## Recommendations for the Caller

To get precise, real-time schedule data, consult:
1. **SpaceX.com/launches** — Official manifest
2. **NextSpaceflight.com** — Community-tracked launch schedule
3. **SpaceFlightNow.com/launch-schedule** — Journalist-curated schedule
4. **r/SpaceX on Reddit** — Launch campaign tracking threads

SpaceX typically averages 2–3 Falcon 9 launches per week in 2026, suggesting 4–8 launches may remain in May 20–31.
Starlink v2 Mini satellite deployment missions constitute 60–70% of SpaceX's launch manifest.
Primary Falcon 9 launch sites: LC-39A and SLC-40 (Cape Canaveral, FL) and SLC-4E (Vandenberg SFB, CA).
Starship/Super Heavy test flights continue from Starbase, Boca Chica, TX, though scheduling is irregular and FAA-dependent.
SpaceX launched 130+ Falcon 9 missions in 2025 and is targeting an even higher cadence in 2026.
Crew Dragon ISS rotation missions occur roughly 3–4 times per year; one may be in the late May/June 2026 timeframe.
Falcon Heavy launches occur roughly 3–5 times per year for heavy-lift government and commercial payloads.
Exact mission names, NET dates, and real-time schedule status for remaining May 2026 could not be confirmed.
